input.background: A trial looked at two explanatory factors in the treatment of hypertension. Factor A was a health-education program aimed at increasing physical activity, improving diet, and lowering body weight. This factor had two levels: active treatment or passive treatment. Factor $B$ was pharmaceutical treatments at three levels: Medication A, Mediation B, and placebo. Because there were two levels of the health-education variable and three levels of pharmacological variable, the experiment evaluated six treatments, as shown in Table 2.2.
input.data:
Table 2.2 Hypertension treatment trial with two factors and six treatments.

\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|l|l|}
\hline \multicolumn{2}{|c|}{\multirow{2}{*}{}} & \multicolumn{3}{|c|}{Factor B} \
\hline & & Medication A & Medication B & Placebo \
\hline \multirow{2}{*}{Factor A Health Education} & Active & 1 & 2 & 3 \
\hline & Passive & 4 & 5 & 6 \
\hline
\end{tabular}
input.question: The response variable was

Factor_A.description: Health education program with two levels: active treatment or passive treatment.

Factor_B.description: Pharmaceutical treatments with three levels: Medication A, Medication B, and placebo.

Treatment_Combination.description: The six treatment combinations resulting from the two factors.
